ops.title: Session-id audit stamping: pack-level field, env precedence, display scope
ops.body: Decision (2026-07-16): every store write (Create/Append/Compact/Merge) stamps the Claude session id as a pack-level 'session' field in the v1 wire (between lamport and ops, omitempty — session-less packs stay byte-identical, so entity ids and goldens are untouched; no packVersion bump, old binaries ignore the key). Env precedence: CC_NOTES_SESSION_ID overrides (set-but-empty suppresses), CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID is the fallback — mirrors CC_NOTES_ACTOR. Display deliberately scoped to history (text 'session:<8 chars>' suffix + full id in --json) and the viz trail panel; no snapshot fields, no fold/FUSE/show-DTO changes. Rejected alternatives: per-op fields (~60 structs + goldens, no extra information), commit-message trailers (new gitobj read plumbing). Merge/bookkeeping commits carry the stamp in the ODB but stay filtered from history by trail's no-visible-diff rule — intentional, not a gap. Test hermeticity: gittest.ScrubEnv + cli gitEnvKeys scrub both vars; fixtures that bypass ScrubEnv (relevantRepo, driftRepoInit) were fixed to call it.
